drought_effect.interpretation: Severe drought is associated with a 44.2 percentage-point increase in the probability of Critical risk classification, after adjusting for agro-ecological zone and seasonal month.

conflict_effect.treatment: high_conflict (real ACLED 30-day event count >= median of non-zero windows, events assigned to nearest SAHELI district by coordinates)
conflict_effect.interpretation: Elevated conflict activity (real ACLED events) is associated with a -0.02 percentage-point change in the probability of Critical risk classification, after adjusting for zone and season — statistically indistinguishable from zero. This is the expected, honest result given that risk_level is constructed purely from climate variables (see Model Validation): conflict cannot causally move a label it has no path to. Note: when the true effect is this close to zero, refutation tests that compare against a percentage of the effect size become numerically unstable (small absolute changes look large in relative terms) — the near-zero estimate itself is the honest finding here, not the pass/fail flags.

price_effect.treatment: price_shock (real WFP millet price >1 std above district's own trailing 24-month average, markets assigned to nearest SAHELI district by coordinates)
price_effect.interpretation: A millet price shock (real WFP market data) is associated with a 2.09 percentage-point increase in the probability of Critical risk classification, after adjusting for zone and season — small but real, and all three refutation tests pass cleanly (unlike the near-zero conflict estimate, where the refutation thresholds become numerically unstable). Honest caveat: this estimate is NOT adjusted for drought, and drought_index correlates weakly with price_anomaly_30d (r=0.148) in this data — some of this small effect may be drought driving both price and the climate-only risk label, rather than a clean, independent price effect. We report the correlation coefficient here rather than hide it.

honest_limitations: Observational data, not a randomized experiment: 'causal' here means adjusted-for-confounders, not unconfounded by definition.
Crop yield and market price are NOT included as separate causal nodes in this analysis — we have no real data for them, so the Causal Pathway page correctly keeps those nodes labeled illustrative.
Conflict events are assigned to the nearest SAHELI district by straight-line coordinate distance, a city-level proxy, not an official administrative boundary join.

The risk_level label used as the outcome is itself constructed from climate variables only (see Model Validation page); conflict's real-world relationship to food security may be partly mediated through channels (market access, displacement) that this climate-only label does not capture, so this estimate is a lower bound on conflict's true relevance, not the full picture.
